name: Espresso-Hazelnut Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
For crust: Preheat oven to 350°F. Generously butter the bottom and sides of a 9-inch diameter springform pan with 2-3/4-inch high sides. Wrap the outside of the pan with a double layer of foil. Finely grind cookies, hazelnuts, sugar, and cinnamon in a food processor. Add melted butter; process until moist clumps form. Press crumb mixture onto bottom and up sides of the pan. Chill while preparing filling.
For filling: In a large bowl, beat cream cheese until smooth using an electric mixer. Add sugar and beat until well blended. Add eggs one at a time, beating just until blended and scraping down the sides of the bowl after each addition. Beat in sour cream and 1/2 cup whipping cream. Dissolve espresso powder with 3/4 cup warm water in a small bowl; stir into filling and beat until blended. Beat in vanilla. Stir in chopped hazelnuts.
Pour filling into prepared pan. Place pan inside a larger baking pan. Pour hot water into the larger pan so it comes halfway up the sides of the springform pan.
Bake cake until top is puffed and center is nearly set, about 1-1/4 hours. Turn off oven; open door slightly. Let cake stand in oven with door ajar for one hour. Remove pan from water and transfer to a rack. Cool completely. Wrap in foil and chill overnight (can be made up to 3 days in advance). Run a knife around the sides of the pan to loosen the cheesecake. Carefully remove pan sides.
Beat remaining whipping cream until stiff peaks form using a large star-tipped bag. Pipe around the top edge. Decorate with chocolate-covered espresso beans.
